config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai tanpa kontrol, yang sering dirugikan adalah orang kecil dan kelompok yang suaranya lemah. Jadi, prioritas yang sehat adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should matter, but I do not think its interests should automatically override individual rights. From my perspective in China, collective goals can bring real benefits like stability, public order, and rapid development, but I have also seen how “for the greater good” can become a convenient excuse to silence ordinary people or ignore unfair treatment. If individual rights are treated as secondary all the time, then the people with less power are the ones who pay the price. A better principle is that society and the individual should be balanced, with clear limits on what collective interests are allowed to do.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai secara mutlak, karena di Indonesia dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana, kesehatan publik, atau ketertiban umum di mana kepentingan bersama bisa membatasi hak individu, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang terjadi justru mayoritas atau negara merasa berhak mengorbankan warga yang suaranya lemah. Jadi, menurut saya hak individu bukan lawan dari kepentingan masyarakat, melainkan batas penting agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah jadi alat kesewenang-wenangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, collective interests can be important for stability, public health, and development, but I have also seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify ignoring ordinary people’s dignity and fairness. Agent A is right that emergency situations may require limits, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable, or else the weaker side will always bear the cost. For me, the real test of a good society is not how much power it can take from individuals, but how well it protects them while still serving the public interest.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembungkaman, atau keputusan yang merugikan warga kecil, padahal mereka paling sedikit punya kuasa untuk melawan. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti bencana atau kesehatan publik yang bisa membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling bebas mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak orang lemah sambil tetap mengejar kepentingan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should generally take priority over individual rights. From my perspective in China, collective goals can produce stability and development, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can become a justification for ignoring fairness, dignity, and the voices of ordinary people. Agent A’s point about emergency limits is valid, but those limits must stay narrow and accountable, because once collective interest becomes a blank check, the people with the least power suffer first. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individuals most easily, but one that protects individual rights while still pursuing the public good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
